channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Ruling overturns Senegal's Africa Cup title and declares Morocco the champion
item.description: Morocco was stunningly awarded the Africa Cup of Nations title on Tuesday by governing body judges who overturned Senegal's victory in a chaotic final in January.
item.content:encoded:
Morocco was stunningly awarded the Africa Cup of Nations title on Tuesday by governing body judges who overturned Senegal's victory in a chaotic final in January.

(Image credit: Youssef Loulidi)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: EU offers to pay Ukraine to fix oil pipeline at the center of Ukraine-Hungary feud
item.description: EU officials say they have offered money and technical help to Ukraine to fix a key oil pipeline to Central Europe. They hope that will persuade Hungary to drop its veto on major aid to Ukraine.
item.content:encoded:
EU officials say they have offered money and technical help to Ukraine to fix a key oil pipeline to Central Europe. They hope that will persuade Hungary to drop its veto on major aid to Ukraine.

(Image credit: Sven Kaestner)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Iran expert says Trump's 'war of choice' has morphed into a 'war of necessity'
item.description: With the Strait of Hormuz blocked, policy expert Karim Sadjadpour says the war in Iran is becoming increasingly complicated: "I don't think President Trump ... understood what he was getting into."
item.content:encoded:
With the Strait of Hormuz blocked, policy expert Karim Sadjadpour says the war in Iran is becoming increasingly complicated: "I don't think President Trump ... understood what he was getting into."

(Image credit: Majid Saeedi)
item.dc:creator: Terry Gross

item.title: Trump lashes out at NATO after countries decline to help in Iran
item.description: President Trump says NATO is making "a very foolish mistake" after countries decline to help secure the Strait of Hormuz.
item.content:encoded: President Trump says NATO is making "a very foolish mistake" after countries decline to help secure the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: Franco Ordoñez

item.title: Afghanistan says at least 400 people are dead in Kabul after Pakistan airstrike
item.description: Afghanistan accuses Pakistan of hitting a hospital in an airstrike that killed hundreds and wounded hundreds more. Pakistan claims its target was a military installation
item.content:encoded: Afghanistan accuses Pakistan of hitting a hospital in an airstrike that killed hundreds and wounded hundreds more. Pakistan claims its target was a military installation
item.dc:creator: Betsy Joles

item.title: Human rights advocates: War with Iran could spark new age of nuclear proliferation
item.description: Could the war with Iran lead to a world with more nuclear weapons, not less? Mary Louise Kelly speaks with Zeid Raad Al Hussein, about a potential new age of nuclear proliferation.
item.content:encoded: Could the war with Iran lead to a world with more nuclear weapons, not less? Mary Louise Kelly speaks with Zeid Raad Al Hussein, about a potential new age of nuclear proliferation.
item.dc:creator: Mary Louise Kelly

item.title: Ukraine strings nets over cities as killer drones turn streets into war zones
item.description: In eastern Ukraine, white nylon nets now stretch over roads and city streets, a low-tech defense against deadly FPV drones that dominate the battlefield and threaten civilians near the front line.
item.content:encoded:
In eastern Ukraine, white nylon nets now stretch over roads and city streets, a low-tech defense against deadly FPV drones that dominate the battlefield and threaten civilians near the front line.

(Image credit: Anton Shtuka for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Eleanor Beardsley

item.title: Joe Kent, a top counterterrorism official, resigns citing Iran war
item.description: Kent said he "cannot in good conscience" back the Iran war. In his resignation letter, he says Iran "posed no imminent threat to our nation."
item.content:encoded:
Kent said he "cannot in good conscience" back the Iran war. In his resignation letter, he says Iran "posed no imminent threat to our nation."

(Image credit: Nathan Howard)
item.dc:creator: NPR Washington Desk

item.title: U.S. seeks NATO help with Strait of Hormuz. And, federal judge blocks vaccine changes
item.description: As the war with Iran intensifies, Trump is demanding that allies help the U.S. reopen the Strait of Hormuz. And, a federal judge halts RFK Jr.'s changes to children's vaccine policies.
item.content:encoded:
As the war with Iran intensifies, Trump is demanding that allies help the U.S. reopen the Strait of Hormuz. And, a federal judge halts RFK Jr.'s changes to children's vaccine policies.

(Image credit: AFP)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: A Trump official quits over the Iran war and Israel kills 2 top Iranian commanders
item.description: U.S. counterterrorism official Joe Kent became the first senior Trump administration member to resign over the Iran war. Israel killed two top Iranian officials, dealing another blow to the regime.
item.content:encoded:
U.S. counterterrorism official Joe Kent became the first senior Trump administration member to resign over the Iran war. Israel killed two top Iranian officials, dealing another blow to the regime.

(Image credit: Vahid Salemi)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff
